title: Recursion, Recurrences, and Divide-and-Conquer · Trace Retrieval
summary: Break a large document and spatial search problem into independently checkable subproblems. Learners retrieve rules and procedures before seeing feedback. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for a recursive solver with recurrence evidence after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Define base cases, progress measures, and combination rules before recursive calls.
Trace call trees, stack depth, repeated subproblems, and returned values.
Write and interpret recurrences for divide, solve, and combine costs.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
